title: Exact Duplicates, Near Duplicates, Change Detection, and Version History · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers exact duplicates, near duplicates, change detection, and version history into a changed multi-signal content identity and change ledger. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of multi-signal content identity and change ledger with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Compute and label wire, decoded, DOM, main-content, record, and rendered digests for distinct questions.
Separate URL aliases, byte-identical responses, equivalent admitted records, and replayed observations without deleting provenance.
Use tokenization, boilerplate policy, thresholds, examples, and false-merge review for similarity decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Stage Specific Digests
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 1307 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate stage digests, exact identity, near-duplicate review, change classes, versions, and tombstones; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
